Ayahs

وَٱلنَّجْمِ إِذَا هَوَىٰ (۱)
By the star when it sets: (1)
مَا ضَلَّ صَاحِبُكُمْ وَمَا غَوَىٰ (۲)
your companion has neither gone astray, nor amiss. (2)
وَمَا يَنطِقُ عَنِ ٱلْهَوَىٰٓ (۳)
Nor does he speak out of [his own] desire: (3)
إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا وَحْىٌ يُوحَىٰ (٤)
it is just a revelation that is revealed [to him], (4)

Key Points

“Hawā” means the inclination towards falling (or descent), and the desires of the self (hawā al-nafs) are the desires that lead a person to fall. “Ḍall” means deviation from the straight path, and “ghawā” refers to ideological deviation arising from a false belief. “Ṣāḥib” means friend or helper. This word has been used several times in the Qur’an regarding the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله (1), because the Prophet’s interaction with people was sincere, compassionate, and friendly. The last word in the previous Surah (At-Tur) was “Nujūm” (stars), and the first word in this Surah is “Najm” (star). The meaning of «و النّجم اذا هَوى» (By the star when it descends) is either the time of sunrise every morning when the light of the stars fades, or the time of the establishment of the Resurrection when the stars fall and set.

In the sphere of Monotheism (Tawhid), Allah and His Prophets are in a position of mutual confirmation. Allah declares the Prophet free from any deviation, «ما ضلّ صاحبكم و ما غوى » (Your companion has not strayed, nor has he erred), and the Prophet likewise declares Allah free from imperfection (tanzīh). In introducing the Qur’an, Allah said: «ذلك الكتاب لاریب فیه» (2) (This is the Book about which there is no doubt), and in introducing the Prophet, He said: «ما ینطق عن الهوى», so that the proof (hujjah) against the people is complete and no room for doubt remains.

The Qur’an addresses the deviants, saying: You were companions and associates of the Prophet for a long time, and you know well that he has no deviation. «ما ضلّ صاحبكم و ما غوى »

The distinction of Divine leaders from human leaders lies in several things: 1. They have no history of intellectual deviation. «ما ضلّ» 2. They have no history of sin or transgression (fisq) in action. «ما غوى» 3. They are compassionate and popular (of the people). «صاحبكم» 4. They are not overcome by their own desires or the desires of others. «ما ینطق عن الهوى»

The late Shaykh Ṣadūq, in his Amālī, regarding the fact that the satisfaction of all people cannot be gained and people's tongues cannot be controlled, states: Some people even attributed [actions based on] personal desire (hawā wa hawas) to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله, and Allah defended the Prophet, saying: «و ما ینطق عن الهوى» (3)

We read in a Hadith that Hamzah, the uncle of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, asked him: Why did you close all the doors that opened into the mosque except the door of the house of Ali عليه السلام? The Prophet recited the first verses of Surah An-Najm: «ان هو الاّ وحىٌ یُوحى » (It is nothing but a revelation revealed), meaning, I did this based on a Divine command (4).

Likewise, when Ali عليه السلام was appointed to the Imamate and successorship (wiṣāyah) at Ghadir Khumm, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله was criticized, but the Prophet recited this verse: «ان هو الا وحى يوحى» (5)

In response to the disbelievers who said: «ان هذا الاّ اساطیر الاولین» (6) (This is nothing but the legends of the former peoples)—that the Qur’an is nothing but the legends of the ancients—Allah decisively states: «ان هو الاّ وحىٌ یوحى » (It is nothing but a revelation revealed). His words are nothing but revelation.

Although the Qur’an is not a book of poetry and its verses do not have the status of poetic couplets, it possesses a special order (naẓm) in its words and phrases, and its recitation is beautiful and rhythmic. «هَوى - غَوى - الهَوى - یُوحى »

1) Saba’, 46; Al-A‘raf, 184; At-Takwir, 22. 2) Al-Baqarah, 2. 3) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq. 4) Tafsir Durr al-Manthūr. 5) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 6) Al-An‘am, 25.

Messages

  1. It is permissible to swear by the manifestations of nature, as they are signs of Allah. «و النّجم», «و الشّمس», «و القمر»
  2. The movement of the stars is towards setting and destruction, and they will disintegrate in the future. «والنّجم اذا هوى»
  3. Slander is common in the path of propagating religion, but Allah defends [His Prophet]. «ما ضلّ صاحبكم و ما غوى»
  4. Prophets lived among deviants for a lifetime but did not deviate, and this itself is a sign of greatness. «ما ضلّ صاحبكم»
  5. The great celestial bodies are perishable, but the Prophet and his words remain firm. «والنّجم اذا هوى . ما ضلّ صاحبكم و ماغوى . و ما ینطق عن الهوى»
  6. The command and speech of the Prophet are authoritative and mandatory to implement. «و ما ینطق عن الهوى»
  7. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله neither deviated in the past, «ما ضلّ», nor will He deviate in the future. «و ما ینطق عن الهوى»
  8. The higher the position and responsibility, the greater the guarantee required for the integrity of the responsible person. «ما ضلّ - ما غوى - ما ینطق»
  9. In leadership, pure individuals must be defended, and their reputation cleared of slander. «ما ضلّ صاحبكم...»
  10. Both the Prophet and his speech are truthful. «ماضلّ صاحبكم... - اِن هو الاّ وحى یوحى»
  11. The Prophet's speech arises neither from personal inclinations nor from the social environment. «اِن هو الاّ وحى یوحى»
  12. The authority over the content, time, place, and quantity of the revelation rests with Allah. «یوحى»
  13. Personal desire (hawā wa hawas) is incompatible with revelation. «و ما ینطق عن الهوى اِن هو الاّ وحى یوحى»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah An-Najm

This Surah has sixty-two verses and was revealed in Mecca. It is one of the Surahs that contains a mandatory prostration (sajdah wājib). The name of the Surah is taken from the first verse, where Allah swears by the star. This Surah refers to the status of revelation and the manner of its descent upon the heart of the Prophet, and, like Surah Al-Isra, discusses the Prophet's ascension (Mi'raj) to the heavens. Another part of the Surah addresses and condemns the superstitious customs of the polytheists regarding idols and the worship of angels. However, the dominant spirit of the Surah is attention to the Day of Judgment and the states of the righteous and the wicked, which serve as the criterion for Divine reward and punishment.

Ayahs

عَلَّمَهُۥ شَدِيدُ ٱلْقُوَىٰ (٥)
taught him by one of great powers, (5)
ذُو مِرَّةٍ فَٱسْتَوَىٰ (٦)
possessed of sound judgement. He settled, (6)
وَهُوَ بِٱلْأُفُقِ ٱلْأَعْلَىٰ (۷)
while he was on the highest horizon. (7)
ثُمَّ دَنَا فَتَدَلَّىٰ (۸)
Then he drew nearer and nearer (8)
فَكَانَ قَابَ قَوْسَيْنِ أَوْ أَدْنَىٰ (۹)
until he was within two bows’ length or even nearer, (9)
فَأَوْحَىٰٓ إِلَىٰ عَبْدِهِۦ مَآ أَوْحَىٰ (۱٠)
whereat He revealed to His servant whatever He revealed. (10)
مَا كَذَبَ ٱلْفُؤَادُ مَا رَأَىٰٓ (۱۱)
The heart did not deny what it saw. (11)

Key Points

“Mirrah” (مِرَّه) means twisted and firm, and it is used in the context of power and ability, as the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: Charity is not permissible for the “Murrah” and the “Sawiyy,” meaning capable individuals. (7) “Danā” (دنى) means he drew near, but “Tadallā” (تدلّى) means he drew very near, to the extent that due to the intensity of the proximity, he became attached. “Qāb” (قاب) is equivalent to the word 'measure' (or amount), and “Qaws” (قوس) means bow. The expression “Qāba Qawsayn” (the measure of two bows' lengths) is a metaphor for the extreme closeness and proximity of the Prophet to Allah during the descent of revelation.

There is discussion and debate regarding whether “Shadīd al-Qawā” (the One Mighty in Power) refers to Allah or to Gabriel, but this title has been used only for Allah in other instances. For example, in Du'a Nudbah we read: “Wa arih Sayyidahu Yā Shadīd al-Qawā” (O Allah! Show us his Master, O Mighty in Power), or in the supplication narrated from Imam Hassan عليه السلام for repelling the evil of the oppressor, we read: “Yā Shadīd al-Qawā Yā Shadīd al-Mihāl” (O Mighty in Power, O Severe in Might). (8) Furthermore, in other verses, Allah is introduced as the Teacher of the Quran, including: «الرّحمن علّم القرآن» (9) The Teacher of the Quran is Allah, the Most Merciful. «علّمك ما لم تكن تعلم» (10), Allah taught you what you did not know. Of course, in Sūrat At-Takwīr, regarding Gabriel, we read: «ذى قوّة» (Possessor of Power) (11), but there is a great difference between “Dhī Quwwah” and “Shadīd al-Qawā.” Therefore, the intent of “Shadīd al-Qawā” is Allah.

These verses refer to the issue of the Prophet’s Mi‘rāj (ascension) صلى الله علیه وآله, a portrait of which we presented in the seventh volume of Tafsir Noor in connection with the first verse of Sūrat Al-Isrā’.

It is stated in Tafsir Rūḥ al-Ma‘ānī: During the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), when two people swore an oath together, they would place their bows side by side, draw the strings, and shoot one arrow together, symbolizing that the two had become one.

The angel is not in the highest horizon (al-Ufuq al-A‘lā), but rather in the clear horizon (al-Ufuq al-Mubīn), and the Prophet is much higher and can observe him. «و لقد رأه بالافق المبین» (12)

Allah has no location, and the intent of al-Ufuq al-A‘lā (the highest region) is not a specific place, but rather the peak of high status and a metaphor for Allah’s comprehensive encompassment and dominance.

Revelation on Earth is mediated: «نزل به روح الامین على قلبك» (13), Gabriel brought the Quran down upon your heart. But here, where the discussion is about the Prophet’s Mi‘rāj, He said: «فاوحى الى عبده ما اوحى» (Then He revealed to His servant what He revealed), meaning that in the station of proximity, there is no intermediary, and even the Archangel has no access there.

Imam Sajjad عليه السلام was asked: What was the reason for the Mi‘rāj of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله? Does Allah have a location? The Imam replied: The Mi‘rāj was so that Allah could show His Prophet the dominion (Malakūt) of the heavens and the wonders of creation and innovation contained therein. (14)

The class that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله attended during the Mi‘rāj was a special class: Location of the class: The highest place. «و هو بالافق الاعلى» Teacher: (Allah) The most powerful Teacher. «علّمه شدید القوى» Content of the lesson: (Revelation) The firmest speech. «ان هو الا وحى یوحى»

The Imam was asked: «هل رأى رسول اللّه ربّه» Did the Messenger of Allah see his Lord during the Mi‘rāj? The Imam replied: «نعم بقلبه رآه» Yes, he observed God with his heart. Then he recited this verse: «ما كذب الفؤاد ما رأى» (15)

Imam Reza عليه السلام said: The verse «لقد رأى من آیات ربّه الكبرى» (16) interprets the verse «ما كذب الفؤاد ما رأى,» meaning that the intent of seeing (ru’yah) in the verse is the observation of Divine signs, not the Essence of Allah.

7) Tafsir Rūḥ al-Ma‘ānī. 8) Mustadrak, Vol. 5, p. 260. 9) Ar-Raḥmān, 1. 10) An-Nisā’, 113. 11) At-Takwīr, 20. 12) At-Takwīr, 23. 13) Ash-Shu‘arā’, 193. 14) Tafsir Kanz ad-Daqā’iq. 15) Tafsir Kanz ad-Daqā’iq. 16) An-Najm, 18.

Messages

  1. Religion is so valuable that its Teacher must be Allah, Who possesses infinite power, «علّمه شدید القوى,» and its student must be a Prophet who is infallible and without error. «ما ضلّ صاحبكم»

  2. Receiving Divine knowledge requires a special capacity. «ما ضلّ صاحبكم و ما غوى... علّمه شدید القوى»

  3. One way to know individuals is by knowing their teacher. «ما ضلّ صاحبكم... علّمه شدید القوى»

  4. Revelation is the same as Divine teachings. «یوحى... علّمه شدید القوى»

  5. Respond to unfair analyses and accusations. In response to those who said: Others taught this Quran to the Prophet, «یعلّمه بشر» (17), Allah says: «علّمه شدید القوى»

  6. The Prophet’s knowledge is from Allah. «علّمه شدید القوى»

  7. Divine knowledge is inseparable from His power. «علّمه شدید القوى»

  8. Only Allah is the Most Powerful; non-Divine powers are weak. «...شدید القوى»

  9. A teacher must be capable in his work. «علّمه شدید القوى»

  10. Through Allah’s teaching, an unlettered person (ummī) can become the teacher of all humanity. «علّمه شدید القوى»

  11. All human beings are students of Allah, «علّم الانسان ما لم یعلم» (18), but the Prophet himself is His special student. «علّمه شدید القوى»

  12. Divine programs for humanity are very firm and complete. «علّمه شدید القوى ذو مرّة...»

  13. Proximity to Allah is step-by-step and gradual. «دنا فتدلّى»

  14. The relationship between teacher and student must be close and intimate. «دنا فتدلّى»

  15. In speech, we should use the vocabulary and literature of the audience and speak in the language of the people. «قاب قوسین» (In Arab culture, the closeness of two bows was a sign of the closeness of their owners.)

  16. Proximity to Allah has no limit for His servants. «او ادنى» (or nearer), because angels cannot reach this.

  17. Men of God, whether on Earth or in the heavens, are servants of Allah. «اسرى بعبده» (19), «فاوحى الى عبده»

  18. Servitude (to Allah) is the foundation for receiving revelation and Divine favor. «فاوحى الى عبده»

  19. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is infallible both in receiving revelation and in conveying it. «ما ینطق عن الهوى... ما كذب الفؤاد ما رأى»

  20. That inner witnessing is authoritative which is confirmed by Allah. «ما كذب الفؤاد ما رأى»

17) An-Naḥl, 103.

18) Al-‘Alaq, 5.

19) Al-Isrā’, 1.

Ayahs

أَفَتُمَـٰرُونَهُۥ عَلَىٰ مَا يَرَىٰ (۱۲)
Will you then dispute with him about what he saw?! (12)
وَلَقَدْ رَءَاهُ نَزْلَةً أُخْرَىٰ (۱۳)
Certainly he saw it yet another time, (13)
عِندَ سِدْرَةِ ٱلْمُنتَهَىٰ (۱٤)
by the Lote Tree of the Ultimate Boundary, (14)
عِندَهَا جَنَّةُ ٱلْمَأْوَىٰٓ (۱٥)
near which is the Garden of the Abode, (15)

Key Points

“Tumārūnah” (تمارونه) is derived from “Mirā’” (مراء), meaning argumentation accompanied by doubt and skepticism. “Nazlah” (نزلة) means descending all at once. “Sidrat al-Muntahā” (سدرة المنتهى) is the name of a place near the Promised Paradise, which is overflowing with Divine favors. According to narrations, no one except the Prophet of Islam Himself has ascended higher than it.(20) It is mentioned in a hadith: The place that marks the final stage of the angels' flight or the final stage of the ascent of deeds is called “Sidrat al-Muntahā.” In narrations, we read: “Sidrat al-Muntahā” is the name of a tree, and with every leaf of it, there is an angel engaged in tasbīh (glorification).(21)

Since there was much disbelief and skepticism regarding the event of the Mi'raj (Ascension), Allah stated once in Sūrat al-Isrā’: «سبحان الّذى» (Glory be to Him Who...), meaning He is far removed from any futile act, and the Mi'raj had a very high purpose. And He stated once in this Sūrah: Why do you argue about this matter? «أفتمارونه...»

Although all people are under the observation of Allah, sometimes Allah gives special attention to certain individuals. «و لقد راه نزلة اخرى» means that Allah gave His Prophet special attention once again during the Mi'raj. Just as Allah is with all people, «و هو معكم»(22) yet His being with the pious is of a different kind. «انّ اللّه مع الّذین اتّقوا»(23) Yes, the companionship and favor of Allah are sometimes general and sometimes specific. He hosted His Prophet in the heavens and granted him His special attention near Sidrat al-Muntahā and the Promised Paradise.

This meaning is mentioned in the Tafsīr Atyab al-Bayān, but in other tafsīrs (commentaries), it has been interpreted differently: that the Prophet saw Gabriel in his original form once again, or that he saw God with his inner vision once again. Our reason for choosing the interpretation of Atyab al-Bayān is that these verses are in the position of glorifying the Prophet and his Mi'raj. Seeing Gabriel is not a distinction or glorification for the Prophet, whose status is higher than Gabriel, and it does not fit well with the context of the verses. Furthermore, the inner vision of Allah was always present for the Prophet, not just once or twice. However, the special favor of Allah during the Mi'raj was noteworthy, because Allah took His Prophet to the heavens with a specific goal, and therefore, He granted him a special gaze. «راه نزلة اخرى»

We read in a hadith that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: I saw Gabriel near Sidrat al-Muntahā. Gabriel told the Prophet: The limit that Allah has set for me is right here, and if I go further than this, my wings will burn.(24)

(20) تفسیر كنزالدقائق. (21) تفاسیر نمونه و مجمع البیان. (22) حدید، ۴. (23) نحل، ۱۲۸. (24) تفسیر هدایت.

Messages

1- Disbelievers lack the tolerance to accept the revelations and experiences of the friends of Allah (Awliyā'), and they engage in argumentation against them with pessimism and doubt. «أفتمارونه»

2- Discussion and doubt are appropriate in theoretical and intellectual matters, but they have no place regarding tangible and visible realities. «أفتمارونه على ما یرى»

3- Universal acceptance is not a prerequisite for propagation (tablīgh); some deny, doubt, or argue. «أفتمارونه»

4- Inquiry and seeking evidence are always recommended, «فسئلوا أهل الذكر»(25) but contention and strife are subject to reproach. «أفتمارونه»

5- Something higher than Paradise also exists. «عند سدرة المنتهى عندها جنة المأوى» Sidrat al-Muntahā is so important that Paradise, despite its grandeur, is located beside it.

(25) نحل، ۴۳.

Ayahs

إِذْ يَغْشَى ٱلسِّدْرَةَ مَا يَغْشَىٰ (۱٦)
when there covered the Lote Tree what covered it. (16)
مَا زَاغَ ٱلْبَصَرُ وَمَا طَغَىٰ (۱۷)
His gaze did not swerve, nor did it overstep the bounds. (17)
لَقَدْ رَأَىٰ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِ ٱلْكُبْرَىٰٓ (۱۸)
Certainly he saw some of the greatest signs of his Lord. (18)

Key Points

In the first Ayah of Surah Al-Isra, we read that Allah caused His Prophet to travel by night so that He might show him His signs: «لِنُریَه من آیاتنا» (that We might show him some of Our signs). In this Surah, He says: Allah showed him His signs. «و لقد رأى من آیات ربّه الكبرى» (And indeed he saw some of the greatest signs of his Lord.) The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is infallible in every respect: a) Thoughts and deeds: «ما ضلّ صاحبكم» b) Tongue and speech: «ما ینطق عن الهوى» c) Knowledge and learning: «علّمه شدید القوى» d) Heart and mind: «ما كذب الفؤاد ما رأى» e) Eye and sight: «ما زاغ البصر و ما طغى»

Messages

1- The right of the eye is that it should neither swerve nor transgress. «ما زاغ البصر و ما طغى»

2- An eye is capable of seeing the great Divine signs only if it is realistic and truth-seeing. «ما زاغ البصر و ما طغى و لقد رأى من آیات ربّه الكبرى»

3- The Divine signs are so vast that even the noblest of creation sees only a part of them. «من آیات ربّه»

Ayahs

أَفَرَءَيْتُمُ ٱللَّـٰتَ وَٱلْعُزَّىٰ (۱۹)
Have you considered Lat and ‘Uzza? (19)
وَمَنَوٰةَ ٱلثَّالِثَةَ ٱلْأُخْرَىٰٓ (۲٠)
and Manat, the third one? (20)
أَلَكُمُ ٱلذَّكَرُ وَلَهُ ٱلْأُنثَىٰ (۲۱)
Are you to have males and He females? (21)
تِلْكَ إِذًا قِسْمَةٌ ضِيزَىٰٓ (۲۲)
That, then, will be an unfair division! (22)

Key Points

Where is the Almighty Allah, Who raises humanity to the heights of the heavens and shows him His great signs, and where are the useless stone idols? The names of nine idols are mentioned in the Qur'an: "لات" (Lat), "عُزّى" (Uzza), and "مناة" (Manat) in this Surah; "بعل" (Ba'l) in Surah As-Saffat; and "ودّ" (Wadd), "سواع" (Suwa'), "یَعوق" (Ya'uq), "یغوث" (Yaghuth), and "نسر" (Nasr) in Surah Nuh.

Messages

1- Observation must be purposeful. «أفرایتم اللات...»

2- The preacher must be aware of the factors and means of deviation. «اللات والعزّى و مناة...»

3- Sometimes, objects, individuals, and factors of deviation must be introduced, exposed, and condemned so that others may learn lessons and be guided. «اللات والعزّى و مناة...»

4- In reform, one must first address the sources. In idol-breaking, one must first target the major idols. (Similar to the Ayah: «فقاتلوا ائمّة الكفر»(26)) «اللات و العزّى و مناة...»

5- Do not approve for others what you do not approve for yourselves. «ألكم الذكر وله الانثى» (The polytheists, who considered daughters a disgrace, called the angels the daughters of Allah.)

6- In guidance, the method of posing questions can be utilized. «ألكم الذكر و له الانثى»

7- Discrimination between daughters and sons is a form of injustice. «ألكم الذكر و له الانثى . تلك اذاً قسمة ضیزى » (26) Tawbah, 12.

Ayah

إِنْ هِىَ إِلَّآ أَسْمَآءٌ سَمَّيْتُمُوهَآ أَنتُمْ وَءَابَآؤُكُم مَّآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ بِهَا مِن سُلْطَـٰنٍ ۚ إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا ٱلظَّنَّ وَمَا تَهْوَى ٱلْأَنفُسُ ۖ وَلَقَدْ جَآءَهُم مِّن رَّبِّهِمُ ٱلْهُدَىٰٓ (۲۳)
These are but names, which you have coined—you and your fathers—for which Allah has not sent down any authority. They follow nothing but conjectures and the desires of the [lower] soul, while there has already come to them the guidance from their Lord. (23)

Key Points

The meaning of "Sultan" (سلطان) in the Quran is proof and evidence, which leads to intellectual dominance. Idolaters considered each idol to be the manifestation of a certain matter; one the manifestation of power, one knowledge, one love, one wrath, and so on. The Quran states: These impressive titles and appellations have no efficacy, and these names are without substance (meaningless).

Messages

1- Attention should not be paid to every slogan, title, or appellation. «أسماء سمّیتموها»

2- Ancestors are the culture-makers for the generation that follows them. «سمّیتموها أنتم و آباءكم»

3- Blind imitation, even if it is of ancestors and fathers, is forbidden. «أنتم و آباءكم»

4- Polytheism (Shirk) arises from fantasy and delusion and lacks any logical foundation. «سمّیتموها... ما أنزل اللّه بها من سلطان»

5- Beliefs must be based on evidence (Burhan); conjecture, speculation, and imitation are not sufficient. «ما أنزل اللّه بها من سلطان ان یتّبعون الاّ الظن»

6- Guidance is from Allah, «جاءهم من ربّهم الهدى», and deviation is from carnal desires (Hawa al-Nafs). «تهوى الانفس»

7- Following desires (Hawa-parasti) is condemned. «یتبعون... ما تهوى الانفس»

8- Allah has completed the proof (Itmam al-Hujjah) for everyone, and after the coming of revelation, man has no further excuse. «و لقد جاءهم من ربّهم الهدى»

9- Guidance and direction are among the functions of Lordship (Rububiyyah). «من ربّهم الهدى»

10- Whenever you condemn certain paths, present the path of truth. «جاءهم من ربّهم الهدى»

11- The path of knowledge and the path of revelation are true. «سلطان... من ربّهم الهدى» But personal preferences «أنتم», ancestors «آباءكم», the path of conjecture and speculation «الا الظنّ», and the path of caprice and desire «تهوى الانفس» are all false.

Ayahs

أَمْ لِلْإِنسَـٰنِ مَا تَمَنَّىٰ (۲٤)
Shall man have whatever he yearns for? (24)
فَلِلَّهِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةُ وَٱلْأُولَىٰ (۲٥)
Yet to Allah belong this world and the Hereafter. (25)
۞ وَكَم مِّن مَّلَكٍ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ لَا تُغْنِى شَفَـٰعَتُهُمْ شَيْـًٔا إِلَّا مِنۢ بَعْدِ أَن يَأْذَنَ ٱللَّهُ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيَرْضَىٰٓ (۲٦)
How many an angel there is in the heavens whose intercession is of not any avail, except after Allah permits whomever He wishes and approves of! (26)

Key Points

'Tamannā' means aspiration (or wish), whether the matter is possible or impossible, logical or baseless. (27) However, 'Rajā' is used for achievable aspirations, 'Tamaʿ' for aspirations whose attainment is near, and 'Amal' for aspirations that are difficult to achieve. (28)

Islam reforms the source of thoughts in order to reform behavior. So that we do not turn to others hoping to gain honor and power, He says: «انّ العزّة للّه جمیعا» (29) All honor belongs to Him. «انّ القوّة للّه جمیعا» (30) All power belongs to Him. In this Ayah, He also says: The Hereafter and the world belong exclusively to Allah, so why do you expect things from others? Existence revolves around the will of Allah, not our wishes.

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «عرفت اللّه بفسخ العزائم و نقض العهود» (31) I recognized Allah through the annulment of firm human resolves and the breaking of their covenants, meaning existence revolves by His will, not by our decisions.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Neither an angel nor a prophet can save you against Divine wrath. Whoever expects intercession (Shafā'ah) must seek Allah's pleasure. (32)

Question: Is intercession not a form of favoritism (pārtī bāzī)?

Answer: Never, because: A) The person who is interceded for must have created the eligibility for intercession through their thought and action. Therefore, unlike favoritism, intercession has law and regulation. B) In intercession, no right is lost, but in favoritism, the rights of others are violated. C) The intercessor expects nothing from the interceded, unlike favoritism, where the person who performs the task has expectations. D) The goal of the intercessor is the salvation of the interceded, but the goal in favoritism is worldly gain. E) Intercession is a means of training and growth, because the interceded establishes a spiritual relationship with the friends of Allah who are the intercessors.

«لمن یشاء» (for whom He wills) can be interpreted in two ways: A) He grants permission to intercede to whomever Allah wills. B) He grants permission to be interceded for to whomever Allah wills.

Question: Does intercession not encourage people to commit sin?

Answer: Never, because firstly, it is not known who will be interceded for, and secondly, does the creation of a medicine to treat poisoning encourage people to become poisoned and consume venom?!

A Look at Aspiration in Narrations and Ayahs

Value of Aspiration: Aspiration is a factor and motivation for movement and growth. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: «لولا الامل ما رضعت والدة ولدها و لا غرس غارس شجرها» (33) If it were not for hope (Amal), no mother would suckle her child, nor would any farmer plant a tree.

Prophet Jesus عليه السلام saw an old man holding a shovel and plowing the ground. He said: O God! Take aspiration away from this man. He saw the old man put the shovel aside and sleep. After a while, Prophet Jesus asked God to return the aspiration to him, and he observed that the old man stood up and began to work. (34)

Controlling Aspiration: Aspirations must be within the framework of possibilities, talents, conditions, and capacity, otherwise they lead to delusions. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: My greatest fear for you is following desires and having lengthy aspirations. (35)

Negative Aspirations: The Quran criticizes and prohibits certain aspirations. Such as those who wished they had the wealth of Qarun and said: «یا لیت لنا مثل ما اوتى قارون» (36) Or they wish to be praised unjustly: «یحبّون أن یحمدوا بما لم یفعلوا» (37) Or they wish to have superiority over others. «تلك الدار الاخرة نجعلها للذین لا یریدون علوّاً فى الارض» (38) Allah has repeatedly restrained the believers from looking longingly at the wealthy and affluent with the phrases: «لاتعجبك اموالهم» (39) and «لاتمدّنّ عینیك» (40).

Aspirations They Will Not Reach: On the Day of Judgment, the criminals will repeatedly say «یا لیتنى» (Oh, I wish!), but what good is it when it is too late, including: «یا لیتنى كنت ترابا» (41) Oh, I wish I were dust. (Dust takes one seed and gives one cluster. Dust takes sewage and gives clear water, but some humans do nothing but corruption.) «یا لیتنى اتّخذت مع الرسول سبیلا» (42) Oh, I wish I had taken a path alongside the Prophet. «یا لیتنى لم اوت كتابیه» (43) Oh, I wish I had not been given my record! «یا لیتنى قدّمت لحیاتى» (44) Oh, I wish I had sent forth something for my life (in the Hereafter)! «یا لیتنا نردّ و لا نكذّب» (45) Oh, I wish we could be returned (to the world) and not deny (the truth) anymore!

Messages

1- Do not abandon the path of Allah hoping for the intercession and problem-solving of others, for you will not reach this aspiration. «ام للانسان ما تمنّى»

2- Abandoning Divine guidance «من ربّهم الهدى» and pursuing imaginary aspirations is condemned. «أم للانسان ما تمنّى»

3- Absolute guardianship (Wilāyah) and sovereignty in the Hereafter and the world belong exclusively to Allah; let us not turn to others besides Him. «فللّه الاخرة و الاولى»

4- Whoever and whatever is besides Allah is transient and not lasting. «فللّه الاخرة و الاولى»

5- The fundamental reality is the wise Will of Allah, whether our aspirations align with it or not. «أم للانسان ما تمنّى فللّه الاخرة و الاولى»

6- The manifestation of Allah's ownership is greater in the Hereafter. «الاخرة و الاولى» (The name of the Hereafter precedes that of the world.)

7- Even the closest angels do not have the power of intercession without Allah's permission, let alone idols. «الا اسماء سمّیتوها... و كم من ملك»

8- Since the Hereafter and the world belong exclusively to Allah, His permission and pleasure are necessary for intercession. «فللّه الاخرة و الاولى... الا ان یاذن اللّه»

9- Neither does every being have the right to intercede, nor is every wrongdoer interceded for. Rather, intercession is for whom Allah wills, and there is no doubt that the Will of Allah is also wise and according to principles and regulations. «...لمن یشاء»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ لَيُسَمُّونَ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ تَسْمِيَةَ ٱلْأُنثَىٰ (۲۷)
Indeed those who do not believe in the Hereafter give female names to the angels. (27)
وَمَا لَهُم بِهِۦ مِنْ عِلْمٍ ۖ إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا ٱلظَّنَّ ۖ وَإِنَّ ٱلظَّنَّ لَا يُغْنِى مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ شَيْـًٔا (۲۸)
They do not have any knowledge of that. They follow nothing but conjectures, and conjecture is no substitute for the truth. (28)

Key Points

Whatever matter is truly scientific (based on knowledge) is truth (Haqq), and every truth corresponds to real knowledge, and both are used interchangeably. Instead of saying, "Conjecture does not dispense man from knowledge," the Quran says, "Conjecture does not dispense man from the truth (Haqq)," thus truth and knowledge are one. «انّ الظنّ لا یغنى من الحق شیئاً» Naming the angels as daughters was based on the false belief that they were the daughters of Allah. Painters who depict angels in the form of girls carry traces of Jahiliyyah (pre-Islamic ignorance) customs, even if unconsciously.

Messages

1- Whoever feels responsibility before the court of Resurrection does not speak every word and does not adopt every guess or conjecture as belief. «لایؤمنون بالاخرة لیسمّون الملائكة»

2- Do not take naming lightly. Those who give inappropriate names to others are subject to reproach. «لیسمّون الملائكة تسمیة الانثى»

3- Whoever lacks knowledge should remain silent. «لیسمّون الملائكة... ما لهم به من علم»

4- Wherever knowledge is absent, guesswork and conjecture prevail. «ما لهم به من علم ان یتّبعون الاّ الظن»

5- In matters of belief, conjecture is not sufficient. «انّ الظنّ لایغنى من الحق شیئاً»

Ayahs

فَأَعْرِضْ عَن مَّن تَوَلَّىٰ عَن ذِكْرِنَا وَلَمْ يُرِدْ إِلَّا ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا (۲۹)
So avoid those who turn away from Our remembrance and desire nothing but the life of the world. (29)
ذَٰلِكَ مَبْلَغُهُم مِّنَ ٱلْعِلْمِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنِ ٱهْتَدَىٰ (۳٠)
That is the ultimate reach of their knowledge. Indeed your Lord knows best those who stray from His way, and He knows best those who are [rightly] guided. (30)

Key Points

In the Quran, the command to turn away (اعراض) from: disbelievers (46), polytheists (47), hypocrites (48), the ignorant (49), those who flee the battlefield (50), and those who delve into the divine verses with the intention of mockery and fault-finding (51) has been mentioned.

Portrait of the World in the Quran

In several verses, the Quran has depicted the portrait of the world and worldly life as follows:

  1. The world is little and insignificant. «متاع الدنیا قلیل» (52)
  2. The world is transient; it is a passage, not a dwelling place. «ما عندكم ینفد» (53)
  3. Worldliness is nothing but diversion and play. «و ما هذه الحیاة الدنیا الاّ لهو و لعب» (54)
  4. The world is a means of deception. «ما الحیاة الدنیا الاّ متاع الغرور» (55)
  5. Worldliness is an obstacle to seeking the Hereafter. «أ رضیتم بالحیاة الدنیا من الاخرة» (56)
  6. The world is captivating. «یا لیت لنا مثل ما أوتى قارون» (57)
  7. In Ayah 20 of Surah Al-Hadid, five characteristics are mentioned for the world, which correspond to the five stages of human life: «واعلموا انّما الحیاة الدنیا لعب و لهو و زینة وتفاخر بینكم و تكاثر...» A) La'b (play). (Childhood) B) Lahw (diversion/amusement). (Adolescence) C) Zinah (adornment/self-beautification). (Youth) D) Tafākhur (boasting/pride). (Middle age) E) Takāthur (accumulation of wealth). (Old age)

Yes, the world is a means, not an end, just as we read in the supplication of the month of Sha'ban: «و لاتجعل الدنیا اكبر همّنا» O God, do not make the world our greatest concern.

Messages

  1. In dealing with those who are world-centered and flee from God, do not be lenient. «فاعرض عن من تولّى عن ذكرنا»

  2. Turn away from those who turn their backs on God's religion and are unwilling to hear the name of Allah and His commands. (In Islam, boycotting opponents and enemies is permissible.) «فاعرض عن من تولّى عن ذكرنا»

  3. Dissociation (Tabarri) from the enemies of Islam is a principle. «فاعرض»

  4. In propagating the religion, do not waste time and opportunity on fruitless matters. «فاعرض...»

  5. Sometimes reciprocity is necessary. One must turn away from the person who turns away from the remembrance of Allah. «فاعرض عن من تولّى عن ذكرنا»

  6. Worldliness and turning away from the remembrance of Allah are inseparable. «تولّى عن ذكرنا و لم یُرد الاّ الحیاة الدنیا»

  7. What is reprehensible is the confinement of thought and action solely to achieving worldly goals; otherwise, natural effort and interest are commendable. «و لم یُرد الا الحیاة...»

  8. Worldliness is a sign of a low level of understanding and knowledge. «لم یُرد الا الحیاة الدنیا... ذلك مبلغهم من العلم»

  9. Those who think of nothing but the worldly life are astray. «لم یرد الاّ الحیاة الدنیا... ضلّ...»

  10. Belief in Allah's knowledge and supervision over human actions is a source of comfort and reassurance for the believers. «هو اعلم بمن ضلّ عن سبیله»

  11. Fleeing from God and being world-centered are the clearest signs of deviation. «تولّى عن ذكرنا و لم یُرد الاّ الحیاة الدنیا... اعلم بمن ضلّ»

  12. Allah knows the deeds and intentions of every single servant. «هو اعلم بمَن ضلّ... هو اعلم بمَن اهتدى»

Ayah

وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ لِيَجْزِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَسَـٰٓـُٔوا۟ بِمَا عَمِلُوا۟ وَيَجْزِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا۟ بِٱلْحُسْنَى (۳۱)
To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth, that He may requite those who do evil for what they have done, and reward those who do good with the best [of rewards]. (31)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we read that some turn away from the remembrance of Allah and have no thought other than this world. This Ayah states: Turning away from the One from Whom everything originates, and in Whose hand is the punishment and reward, is the height of foolishness. Even if you seek this world, you should not turn away from Him, because this world also belongs to Him. The greatness of the heavens is greater than that of the earth, hence in the Quran, "السّموات" (the heavens) is always mentioned before "الارض" (the earth).

Messages

1- Just punishment and reward require knowledge and power. «للّه ما فى السّموات... لیجزى»

2- Existence has a purpose. «للّه ما فى السّموات... لیجزى...»

3- Divine reward is superior to man's good deed, but punishment is no more than the bad deed. «الّذین اساءوا بما عملوا... الّذین أحسنوا بالحسنى»

4- Man's destiny is in his own hands. «بما عملوا... الّذین أحسنوا بالحسنى»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَجْتَنِبُونَ كَبَـٰٓئِرَ ٱلْإِثْمِ وَٱلْفَوَٰحِشَ إِلَّا ٱللَّمَمَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ وَٰسِعُ ٱلْمَغْفِرَةِ ۚ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِكُمْ إِذْ أَنشَأَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَإِذْ أَنتُمْ أَجِنَّةٌ فِى بُطُونِ أُمَّهَـٰتِكُمْ ۖ فَلَا تُزَكُّوٓا۟ أَنفُسَكُمْ ۖ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنِ ٱتَّقَىٰٓ (۳۲)
Those who avoid major sins and indecencies, apart from [minor and occasional] lapses. Indeed your Lord is expansive in [His] forgiveness. He knows you best since [the time] He produced you from the earth and since you were foetuses in the bellies of your mothers. So do not flaunt your piety: He knows best those who are Godwary. (32)

Key Points

Fawāḥish (فواحش) is the plural of Fāḥishah (فاحشة) and refers to any sin, such as adultery (Zinā).

Various meanings have been narrated for the word Lamam (لَمَم) in narrations (58), lexicography books, and commentaries, including: 1. A sin that is unintentional and without persistence. 2. A sin that was intended but not carried out. 3. A sin for which an apology and repentance have occurred afterward. 4. A sin for which the promise of punishment has not been given. 5. A sin for the commission of which no fixed penalty (Ḥadd) has been determined.

The issue of major and minor sins has been raised repeatedly in the Quran (59), and that all human deeds are recorded: «كل صغیر و كبیر مستطر» (60). The record of a person's deeds will be opened on the Day of Judgment: «و نخرج له یوم القیامة كتاباً یلقاه منشوراً» (61). At that moment, his cry will be raised: «یا ویلتنا ما لهذا الكتاب لایغادر صغیرة و لاكبیرة الا احصاها» (62) (Woe to us! What is this book that has not left out any small or large deed without recording it?).

Imam Reza عليه السلام listed the major sins (Kabā’ir) as follows: murder, adultery (Zinā), theft, drinking alcohol, parental disobedience and displeasure, fleeing the battlefield, unjustly seizing the property of an orphan, eating carrion, blood, pork, and whatever has been slaughtered in a name other than Allah’s, usury (Ribā), bribery, gambling, short-changing (in measure/weight), slandering chaste women, sodomy (Liwāṭ), false testimony, despairing of Allah’s mercy, feeling secure from divine punishment, hopelessness regarding divine mercy, aiding and relying on oppressors, false oath, withholding the rights of others, lying, arrogance, extravagance (Isrāf), wastefulness (Tabdhīr), treachery, negligence in performing Hajj, enmity towards the friends of Allah (Awliyā’ Allāh), engaging in vain amusements and play (Lahw wa La‘ib), and persistence in sin (63).

Imam Baqir عليه السلام, regarding the phrase «فلاتزكّوا انفسكم» (Do not purify/praise yourselves), said: Do not boast about your prayers, Zakat, fasting, and acts of worship, for Allah knows the people of piety better (64).

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام was asked: Is it permissible for someone to praise and commend himself? He replied: If there is a necessity, there is no objection. Then He said: Have you not heard that Joseph praised himself before the ruler of Egypt, saying: «اجعلنى على خزائن الارض انّى حفیظ علیم» (65) (Appoint me over the treasuries of the land, for I am a knowing guardian of the public treasury)?

Imam Ali عليه السلام, when He heard a group boasting to each other, saying, "We prayed all night until morning, and we fasted yesterday," said: "I slept last night and I did not fast yesterday." Therefore, sometimes one must speak in this manner to prevent one's own or others' arrogance.

A Jewish man came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله and boasted about the miracles of Prophet Moses to the Prophet. The Prophet said: It is improper for a person to praise himself, but for your clarification, I tell you: Prophet Adam, for his repentance; Prophet Noah, for salvation from drowning; Prophet Abraham, for salvation from the fire; and Prophet Moses, for salvation from fear, swore by Allah by the right of Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad علیهم السلام. O Jew! If Moses had seen me, he would have believed in me. Imam Mahdi is from my lineage, and Prophet Jesus will descend from the heavens to aid Him and will pray behind Him.

Notes regarding Sin and Disobedience The importance of disobeying Allah's command is great, regardless of what it is. Nevertheless, not all sins are on the same level. Some sins, such as lying and backbiting, have greater ugliness and incur harsher punishment. Furthermore, the time of the sin, the place of the sin, the intention of the sinner, his knowledge or ignorance regarding the sin, and persistence or lack of persistence in the sin also affect it.

The worth of every human being is measured by the extent to which he resists sin. If we commit a sin for one mithqāl (measure) of gold, our worth is one mithqāl; and if it is for one hundred mithqāls, our worth is one hundred mithqāls.

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: By Allah, if they were to give me the entire earth (the seven climes) so that I might commit a sin to take a husk of barley from the mouth of an ant, I would not do it (66).

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: The worst sin is the sin that its perpetrator considers light or small. «اشدّ الذنوب ما استخف به صاحبه» (67)

We read in the narrations: Indeed, the friend (walī) of Muhammad is he who obeys Allah, even if his kinship is distant; and the enemy of Muhammad is he who disobeys Allah, even if his relationship is close. «انّ ولىّ محمّد مَن اطاع اللّه و اِن بعدت لحمته و انّ عدوّ محمّد مَن عصى اللّه و اِن قَرُبت قرابته» (68)

Transformation of a Minor Sin into a Major Sin Certain factors and conditions transform minor sins into major sins, including: persistence in the minor sin, considering the sin light, rejoicing while committing the sin, committing the sin out of rebellion, being deceived by divine respite, committing the sin openly, and the sin of great and prominent individuals.

Effects of Sin Hardness of heart, removal of blessings, rejection of supplication (Du‘ā), change in sustenance (Rizq), deprivation from certain acts of worship such as the night prayer, sudden calamity, cessation of rain, destruction of homes, disgrace and humiliation, shortening of life, earthquake, poverty and destitution, grief and illness, and the dominance of the wicked. Verses and narrations have been cited for each of these effects.

Ways to Compensate for Sin 1. Prayer. «اقم الصلاة... انّ الحسنات یذهبن السّیئات» (69) 2. Seeking forgiveness for oneself and others (intercession/Shifā‘ah). «فاستغفروا اللّه و استغفر لهم الرسول» (70) 3. Spending (in charity/Infāq). «صدقة السرّ تكفّر الخطیئة» (71) 4. Solving people's problems. «من كفّارات الذنوب العظام اغاثة الملهوف» (72) 5. Faith and good deeds. «و الّذین آمنوا و عملوا الصّالحات لنكفّرنّ عنهم سیّئاتهم» (73) 6. Avoiding major sins. «ان تجتنبوا كبائر... نكفّر عنكم» (74) 7. Repentance and compensating for bad deeds. «الاّ مَن تاب و آمن و عمل عملاً صالحا فاولئك یبدّل اللّه سیّئاتهم حسنات» (75) 8. Participation in war, Jihad, and martyrdom. «و قاتلوا و قتلوا لاكفّرنّ عنهم سیئاتهم» (76) 9. Giving salutations (Salām), feeding people, and the night prayer. «ثلاث كفّارات: افشاء السّلام و اطعام الطّعام و الصلاة بالّیل و النّاس نیام» (77) 10. Reciting Salawāt (blessings) upon Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad. «من لم یقدر على ما یكفر به ذنوبه فلیكثر من الصلوات على محمد و آل محمد فانها تهدم الذنوب ذنبا» (78)

The matter of self-purification (Tazkiyah) and self-praise is separate from the matter of recounting divine blessings. Allah says in this verse: Do not praise yourselves, «فلاتزكّوا أنفسكم», but in another place, He says: «و امّا بنعمة ربّك فحدّث» (79) (But as for the favor of your Lord, report it).

Messages

  1. We should introduce the righteous by stating their characteristics. «الّذین أحسنوا بالحسنى... الّذین یجتنبون»
  2. Performing good deeds is valuable when accompanied by piety and avoidance of sin. «أحسنوا... یجتنبون الكبائر»
  3. Avoiding sin must be a continuous practice and a characteristic of the person. (Yajtanibūn - the present tense verb indicates continuity and permanence.)
  4. The clearest sign of the virtuous is avoiding major sins (Kabā’ir) and sins that have greater ugliness (Qubḥ). «الّذین أحسنوا... الّذین یجتنبون كبائر الاثم و الفواحش»
  5. Approaching sin leads a person into sin. (Therefore, the Quran uses the word Ijtināb (اجتناب), meaning avoidance and distance from sin.) «یجتنبون كبائر»
  6. Do not look for an angel on earth; man is fallible, and even the virtuous are prone to slips and errors. «الّذین أحسنوا... الّذین یجتنبون... الاّ اللّمم»
  7. An educator must overlook faults and forgive certain slips. «الاّ اللّمم»
  8. Sins that are committed momentarily and without obstinacy or persistence must be forgiven. «الاّ اللّمم»
  9. Motivation affects the deed. Sins that are not committed with the motive of rebellion and disobedience are subject to pardon. «الاّ اللّمم»
  10. Divine forgiveness is not a reason or justification for committing sin; in any case, we must avoid sin. «یجتنبون... واسع المغفرة»
  11. Even those who commit major sins should not despair of divine mercy. «انّ ربّك واسع المغفرة»
  12. Allah possesses both knowledge and power, yet He is also the Concealer of faults. «واسع المغفرة هو أعلم بكم اذ أنشأكم من الارض»
  13. Allah knows not only our present state but also our humble embryonic stage; therefore, let us not boast. «اذ أنتم أجنّة فى بطون أمّهاتكم»
  14. Those who avoid sin should not fall into self-praise. «یجتنبون الكبائر... فلاتزكّوا أنفسكم»
  15. Attention to one's former insignificance, weakness, and lowliness prevents a person from self-praise. «اذ أنتم اجنّة فى بطون أمّهاتكم فلاتزكّوا أنفسكم»
  16. Reforming people's behavior must be achieved through reforming their beliefs. The belief in Allah's knowledge and supervision prevents a person from self-praise. «هو أعلم... فلاتزكّوا انفسكم»
  17. If we possess any perfection, it is all from Him; why do we boast about ourselves? «أنشأكم من الارض... فلاتزكّوا أنفسكم»
  18. A person does not become pious merely by feigning piety. «هو أعلم بمن اتّقى»

Ayahs

أَفَرَءَيْتَ ٱلَّذِى تَوَلَّىٰ (۳۳)
Did you see him who turned away, (33)
وَأَعْطَىٰ قَلِيلًا وَأَكْدَىٰٓ (۳٤)
gave a little and held off? (34)
أَعِندَهُۥ عِلْمُ ٱلْغَيْبِ فَهُوَ يَرَىٰٓ (۳٥)
Does he have the knowledge of the Unseen so that he sees? (35)
أَمْ لَمْ يُنَبَّأْ بِمَا فِى صُحُفِ مُوسَىٰ (۳٦)
Has he not been informed of what is in the scriptures of Moses, (36)
وَإِبْرَٰهِيمَ ٱلَّذِى وَفَّىٰٓ (۳۷)
and of Abraham, who fulfilled [his summons]: (37)

Key Points

«أكدى» (Akdā) derives from «كُدیة» (Kudyah), meaning stinginess and ceasing to spend (infaq), which originally means hard ground. «وفىّ» (Waffā) means that Prophet Ibrahim (Abraham) fulfilled his promise and commitment in all matters. Regarding the occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzūl) of the first Ayah, it is said: These verses concern a person who was accustomed to spending (infaq) and helping people. Others told him: "You will become needy in the future." He stopped spending and became afflicted with stinginess. Allah has praised Prophet Ibrahim with various attributes in the Quran, including: Awwāh (oft-imploring), Halīm (forbearing), Basīr (discerning), Mukhlis (sincere), Qānit (obedient), Shākir (grateful), Siddīq (truthful), Mu'min (believer), Muhsin (doer of good), Wafī (faithful), Khalīl (friend), Imām (leader), Uswah (model), Ummah (nation/exemplar), 'Ābid (worshipper), Hanīf (upright), and Muslim (submissive). In the Quran, the Scrolls (Suhuf) of Ibrahim and Musa (Moses) are mentioned together twice: once in this Surah and once in Surah Al-A'lā, Ayah 19. The mention of the "Scrolls of Ibrahim and Musa" is perhaps because Prophet Musa was accepted by the People of the Book (Ahl al-Kitāb), and Prophet Ibrahim was accepted by the polytheists (Mushrikūn) of Mecca. Therefore, the names of these two great figures are mentioned side by side. In contrast to stinginess, the discussion turns to the faithfulness and self-sacrifice (īthār) of Prophet Ibrahim, who gave everything He had in the way of Allah: «بذل نفسه للنیران و قلبه للرحمن و ولده للقربان و ماله للاخوان» (He offered his soul to the fires, his heart to the Most Merciful, his son for sacrifice, and his wealth for his brethren in faith).

Messages

1- In education and training, use accessible role models and examples. «أَفَرَأَيْتَ...»

2- Both inclination and heartfelt interest are necessary, as is extensive work and its continuity; the absence of any one of these is a defect. («تَوَلَّى» (turned away) indicates lack of inclination, «أَعْطَى قَلِيلًا» (gave a little) is a criticism of insufficient effort, and «أَكْدَى» (held back) refers to lack of continuity.)

3- In education, the names of individuals are not important; describing their character and traits is important. «الَّذِي تَوَلَّى... أَعْطَى... أَكْدَى»

4- Both the ascent and the fall of man have stages; the first stage is ideological (belief), and then practical (action). «تَوَلَّى... أَعْطَى قَلِيلًا»

5- A person who is poor and spends a small amount is praiseworthy, «لَا يَجِدُونَ إِلَّا جُهْدَهُمْ» (80) but if a wealthy and affluent person does not help significantly, he deserves reproach. «أَعْطَى قَلِيلًا»

6- Giving little (infaq) is sometimes wise, such as giving a small amount of food to a child, which would cause harm if excessive. But sometimes its root is stinginess, which is subject to criticism. «أَعْطَى قَلِيلًا وَ أَكْدَى»

7- The cure for stinginess is the remembrance of the generous and the self-sacrificing. «أَعْطَى قَلِيلًا وَ أَكْدَى... إِبْرَاهِيمَ الَّذِي وَفَّى»

8- How do you know that you will become poor in the future, causing you to be stingy now? «أَعْطَى قَلِيلًا وَ أَكْدَى أَعِنْدَهُ عِلْمُ الْغَيْبِ فَهُوَ يَرَى»

9- Although distortions have occurred in the heavenly scriptures, parts of them are still reliable. «أَمْ لَمْ يُنَبَّأْ بِمَا فِي صُحُفِ مُوسَى»

10- Heavenly religions share common principles. «أَمْ لَمْ يُنَبَّأْ بِمَا فِي صُحُفِ مُوسَى وَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ»

11- We should honor others by mentioning their perfections and services. «مُوسَى وَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ»

12- In propagation and guidance, we should use accepted examples. «مُوسَى وَ إِبْرَاهِيمَ» (These two Prophets were well-known and recognized during the era of the Prophet's mission.)

13- In education, both positive and negative role models must be presented. «أَعْطَى قَلِيلًا... إِبْرَاهِيمَ الَّذِي وَفَّى»

14- Faithfulness (Wafā) in the path of truth is among the greatest perfections. «وَفَّى»

Ayahs

أَلَّا تَزِرُ وَازِرَةٌ وِزْرَ أُخْرَىٰ (۳۸)
that no bearer shall bear another’s burden, (38)
وَأَن لَّيْسَ لِلْإِنسَـٰنِ إِلَّا مَا سَعَىٰ (۳۹)
that nothing belongs to man except what he strives for, (39)
وَأَنَّ سَعْيَهُۥ سَوْفَ يُرَىٰ (٤٠)
and that he will soon be shown his endeavour, (40)
ثُمَّ يُجْزَىٰهُ ٱلْجَزَآءَ ٱلْأَوْفَىٰ (٤۱)
then he will be rewarded for it with the fullest reward; (41)

Key Points

Verses 38 to 54 contain the teachings of the Books of Abraham and Moses, which are accepted by Islam. Wizr means a heavy burden, and it is also applied to sin, which is, in reality, a heavy burden. A Wazir (Minister) is someone who carries the heavy burden of administering the state. Allah erases evil deeds through good deeds, or He forgives them through repentance (tawbah) and seeking forgiveness (istighfar), or He transforms evil deeds into good ones. However, He does not unjustly place the burden of one person's sin upon another.

When the second Caliph ordered a pregnant woman who had committed adultery to be stoned, Imam Ali عليه السلام said: What sin has the child in her womb committed? Relying on this verse, «و لاتزر وازرة وزر اخرى» (No bearer of burdens shall bear the burden of another), he said: Wait until the ruling is executed after she gives birth.(81) The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: The sin of the adulterous parents is not borne by the child born of adultery, and then He recited the verse «لاتزر وازرة...» (No bearer of burdens...).(82)

Question: The verse «و أن لیس للانسان الاّ ما سعى» (And that man shall have nothing but what he strives for) states that man has no share except what he has striven for, whereas in other verses and narrations we read that man sometimes enters Paradise through intercession (shafa'ah).

Answer: Firstly, this verse is a rejection of the ignorant pre-Islamic notion where people would say to one another: If you give me such-and-such an amount, I will take the burden of your sin upon myself. The Noble Qur'an stated: No one takes the burden of another's sin, and only his own work and effort are recorded for everyone.

Secondly, the verse refers to Divine Justice (‘adl) and not Divine Grace (fadl). That is, according to justice, the punishment and reward of every person must be commensurate with his effort, but Allah, out of His Grace, can bestow favor, give a greater reward, or lessen the punishment. «و یزیدهم من فضله» (And He increases them from His Grace).(83)

Thirdly: If we read in narrations that whoever establishes a good or bad tradition (sunnah) in society shares in the reward or punishment of all those who follow that path, this meaning does not contradict the verse «لیس للانسان الا ما سعى» (Man shall have nothing but what he strives for). This is because the originator of the path of truth or falsehood has also indirectly made an effort, whether through guidance and instruction, or by creating prerequisites and facilities, or through propaganda and encouragement, or through other material and spiritual support. Benefiting from that reward is not without reason, just as a person who was not on the correct path would not be included in intercession.

We read in narrations that after death, a person benefits from the good deeds of his righteous child, or from a book or endowments (waqf) left behind by him.(84) Or, in Paradise, a person's righteous descendants will join him.(85) Or, he benefits from the good deed he commissioned or bequeathed.(86) In all these cases, although the person did not strive or exert effort directly, he played a role indirectly through raising a righteous child, making an endowment (waqf), making a bequest (wasiyyah), or leaving behind a good book.

81) Bihar, Vol. 76, p. 49. 82) Tafasir Rahnamā and Durr al-Manthūr. 83) Nisa, 173. 84) Khisal, p. 151. 85) Tur, 21. 86) Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 315.

Messages

1- Divine punishment and reward are just. «لاتزر وازرة وزر اُخرى»

2- Do not involve yourself in wrongdoing relying on others, for they will not carry your burden. «لاتزر وازرة وزر اُخرى»

3- In dismissals and appointments, do not attribute the deviations of relatives and close associates to the people. «لا تزر وازرة وزر اُخرى»

4- Existence is a field for effort, work, and achieving results. «و أن لیس للانسان الاّ ما سعى»

5- We are obligated to perform our duty, not to achieve the result. Effort and striving are our duty. «الاّ ما سعى»

6- Belief in the permanence of deeds and the just system of reward and punishment both encourages and motivates man, and makes him cautious and responsible regarding his behavior. «و انّ سعیه سوف یرى»

7- No deed is erased from existence. «سعیه سوف یرى»

8- Do not rush to receive the reward for your deeds, O doers of good. «سوف یرى»

9- This world does not have the capacity for complete recompense; there must be a Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) so that justice is fully rendered. «ثم یجزاه الجزاء الاوفى»

10- Man's action is answered with better rewards. «وَفّى... أوفى»

11- Although there are punishments in this world, the complete punishment is in the Hereafter. «الجزاء الاوفى»

12- The Day of Judgment has multiple stages and stations. (It is inferred from the words «سوف» (soon) and «ثمّ» (then) that the stage of witnessing the deed is different from the stage of recompense for the deed.)

Ayahs

وَأَنَّ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ ٱلْمُنتَهَىٰ (٤۲)
that the terminus is toward your Lord, (42)
وَأَنَّهُۥ هُوَ أَضْحَكَ وَأَبْكَىٰ (٤۳)
that it is He who makes [men] laugh, and weep, (43)
وَأَنَّهُۥ هُوَ أَمَاتَ وَأَحْيَا (٤٤)
that it is He who brings death and gives life, (44)

Key Points

Although divine actions occur through intermediaries, in reality, He Himself is the ultimate Doer of everything. The repetition of the pronoun "هو" (He) indicates that Allah alone is the Doer of everything. The contradiction between death and life, or crying and laughing, exists in the view of humans, but in the sight of Allah, all these are good, and there is no contradiction between them.

Messages

1- Neither should the believers become weary nor the disbelievers become arrogant; the end of everyone and everything is in His hand, and He will hold everyone accountable. «الى ربّك المنتهى»

2- Existence is moving towards Him. «الى ربّك المنتهى»

3- Both punishment and reward are in His hand, «سوف یرى», as are emotional states, moods, sorrow, and joy. «أضحك و أبكى»

4- Laughter and crying are both necessary, and one should not prevent laughter and crying during emotional and sensitive states. «أضحك و أبكى»

5- Allah's power is uniform regarding contradictory actions. «أضحك وأبكى، أمات و أحیا»

6- The Day of Judgment is the day of laughter for one group and crying for another group of people. «ثمّ یجزاه الجزاء الاوفى ... انّه هو أضحك و أبكى»

7- Death and life are manifestations of Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ربّك المنتهى... هو أمات و أحیا»

8- The educational method of the Quran is such that it pursues the direction of Tawhid (monotheism) even in contradictory matters. «هو أضحك و أبكى... هو أمات و أحیا»

Ayahs

وَأَنَّهُۥ خَلَقَ ٱلزَّوْجَيْنِ ٱلذَّكَرَ وَٱلْأُنثَىٰ (٤٥)
that it is He who created the mates, the male and the female, (45)
مِن نُّطْفَةٍ إِذَا تُمْنَىٰ (٤٦)
from a drop of [seminal] fluid when emitted; (46)
وَأَنَّ عَلَيْهِ ٱلنَّشْأَةَ ٱلْأُخْرَىٰ (٤۷)
that with Him lies the second genesis, (47)
وَأَنَّهُۥ هُوَ أَغْنَىٰ وَأَقْنَىٰ (٤۸)
that it is He who enriches and grants possessions, (48)
وَأَنَّهُۥ هُوَ رَبُّ ٱلشِّعْرَىٰ (٤۹)
that it is He who is the Lord of Sirius; (49)

Key Points

«أغنى» (He enriched) is derived from «غِنى » (wealth/independence) meaning self-sufficiency, and «أقنى» (He provided) is derived from «قُنیه» (stored wealth) meaning the accumulation of wealth. (87) Of course, some have taken it to mean making poor. (88) Imam Ali عليه السلام, in interpreting the Ayah «أغنى و أقنى », said: Allah both makes one self-sufficient and makes the person content with that very income. (89)

«شِعرى » (Sirius) is the name of a star that was worshipped by a group who imagined that this star influenced their poverty and wealth. The Quran states: That star is governed (marbūb) and under the command of God, not an influencer in your lives. Of course, some have considered شِعرى to be the name of an idol. (90)

Pairing (Zaujiyyah) is the secret to the survival of existence; if we remove pairing, life becomes cold and spiritless and will reach a dead end.

87) Mufradāt Rāghib. 88) Qāmūs al-Qur’ān. 89) Tafsīr Qummi. 90) Tafsīr Namūneh.

Messages

1- Allah has established the law of pairing (Zaujiyyah) in the system of creation, and it is not a contractual matter among human beings. «انّه خلق الزوجین الذكر و الانثى»

2- Implementing contradictory designs upon a single material element is a sign of Allah's power and Lordship. «أضحك و أبكى، أمات و أحیا، الذكر و الانثى»

3- The creation of male and female is both among the wonders and marvels, and among the necessities of life. «الذكر و الانثى»

4- Man and woman are homogeneous in the initial element of creation. «من نطفة»

5- The God Who creates a living being from a drop of water can revive them again from the dead particles of earth. «من نطفة اذا تمنى و انّ علیه النشّأة الاخرى»

6- Reviving the dead and establishing the Resurrection is incumbent upon Allah. «انّ علیه النشأة الاخرى»

7- Both the origin of creation is from Him, «خلق», and the diversity of creation, «الزوجین». He also created the source of creation, «نطفة», and His creation is repeated and continuous, «النشأة الاخرى». And the management of the creatures' present and future is in His hands. «أغنى و أقنى»

8- We should seek self-sufficiency from Him, not from others. «هو اغنى»

9- Now that need and self-sufficiency are in His hands, why the stinginess, arrogance, and seeking needs from others besides Him? «هو أغنى و اقنى»

10- Allah's management extends both over the semen in the womb and over the star in the sky. «نطفة... ربّ الشعرى»

Ayahs

وَأَنَّهُۥٓ أَهْلَكَ عَادًا ٱلْأُولَىٰ (٥٠)
that it is He who destroyed the former ‘Ad, (50)
وَثَمُودَا۟ فَمَآ أَبْقَىٰ (٥۱)
and Thamud, sparing none [of them]; (51)
وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍ مِّن قَبْلُ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ هُمْ أَظْلَمَ وَأَطْغَىٰ (٥۲)
and the people of Noah before that; indeed they were more unjust and rebellious; (52)
وَٱلْمُؤْتَفِكَةَ أَهْوَىٰ (٥۳)
and He overthrew the town that was overturned, (53)
فَغَشَّىٰهَا مَا غَشَّىٰ (٥٤)
covering it with what covered it. (54)
فَبِأَىِّ ءَالَآءِ رَبِّكَ تَتَمَارَىٰ (٥٥)
Then which of the bounties of your Lord will you dispute? (55)
هَـٰذَا نَذِيرٌ مِّنَ ٱلنُّذُرِ ٱلْأُولَىٰٓ (٥٦)
This is a warner, [in the tradition] of the warners of old. (56)

Key Points

«مؤتفكة» (Mu'tafikah) is derived from «ائتفاك» (i'tifāk), meaning overturned and transformed. Since the cities where the people of 'Ād resided were overturned and collapsed due to the punishment, this title was applied to them. «أهوى» (Ahwā) means to drop or collapse, and «تتمارى» (tatamārā) means argumentation accompanied by the creation of doubt and skepticism. The phrase «انهم كانوا هم أظلم و أطغى» (Indeed, they were the most unjust and the most rebellious) might relate to the people of Noah, who were more unjust than 'Ād and Thamūd, or it might relate to the people of 'Ād, Thamūd, and Noah collectively, meaning these three nations were more unjust and rebellious than the other destroyed nations.

Messages

  1. Punishments are not restricted to the Day of Judgment; Allah destroys some nations in this world. «أهلك عاداً الاولى»
  2. We must learn lessons from the evil fate and destruction of deviant predecessors. «أهلك عاداً... و ثمود... و قوم نوح»
  3. Injustice (ẓulm) and rebellion (ṭughyān), especially their continuation, are factors leading to destruction. «اهلك... كانوا هم أظلم و أطغى »
  4. When studying history, its events, and its transformations, attention must be paid to their causes and philosophy. «كانوا هم أظلم و أطغى »
  5. The destruction of oppressors is one of Allah's blessings and favors. «فباىّ آلاء ربّك»
  6. The primary duty of all Prophets and heavenly books has been warning and admonition. «هذا نذیر من النذر الاولى»

Ayahs

أَزِفَتِ ٱلْـَٔازِفَةُ (٥۷)
The Imminent [Hour] is near at hand. (57)
لَيْسَ لَهَا مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ كَاشِفَةٌ (٥۸)
There is none who may unveil it besides Allah. (58)
أَفَمِنْ هَـٰذَا ٱلْحَدِيثِ تَعْجَبُونَ (٥۹)
Will you then wonder at this discourse, (59)
وَتَضْحَكُونَ وَلَا تَبْكُونَ (٦٠)
and laugh and not weep, (60)
وَأَنتُمْ سَـٰمِدُونَ (٦۱)
while you remain heedless?! (61)
فَٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِلَّهِ وَٱعْبُدُوا۟ ۩ (٦۲)
So prostrate yourselves to Allah and worship Him! (62)

Key Points

"Āzifah" (آزفة) means that which is approaching, and "sāmid" (سامد) means heedlessness, negligence, and arrogance. We read in a Hadith in Tafsir Furqan that after the revelation of these verses, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله did not laugh until the end of his life, only smiling. Furthermore, the last verse requires an obligatory prostration (Sajdah Wajib). Perhaps the intent of "هذا الحدیث" (this discourse/narration) is the Qur'an, just as the Qur'an is referred to as "Hadith" in verse 23 of Surah Az-Zumar: "اللّه نزّل أحسن الحدیث" (Allah has sent down the best discourse).

Messages

1- Let us not consider the Day of Judgment far off. "ازفتِ الآزفة"

2- All means and resources cannot alleviate even an iota of the distresses of the Day of Judgment. "لیس لها من دون اللّه كاشفة"

3- One should be astonished at the astonishment of some people regarding the Day of Judgment. "تعجبون"

4- Whoever believes in the Day of Judgment reduces his laughter and weeps in remembrance of the Resurrection. "و تضحكون و لاتبكون"

5- Sometimes laughter is a sign of arrogance and intoxication (with worldly life). "تضحكون... و أنتم سامدون"

6- The remedy for pride and intoxication is prostration (Sajdah) and worship. "فاسجدوا... و اعبدوا"
